text:
```yaml infra-staging-egreen/workspace/ftp.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-config
data:
  SFTPGO_FTPD__BINDINGS__0__PORT: "2121"
  SFTPGO_FTPD__PASSIVE_PORT_RANGE__START: "30000"
  SFTPGO_FTPD__PASSIVE_PORT_RANGE__END: "30005"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__DRIVER: "postgresql"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__NAME: "sftpgo"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__HOST: "db-sftpgo"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__USERNAME: "sftpgo"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__PASSWORD: "sftpgociw8sWECsfgdd29fH2nwhkB"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__POOL_SIZE: "10"
  SFTPGO_HTTPD__BRANDING__NAME: "eGreen FTP server"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-secret
type: Opaque
data:
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__PASSWORD: c2Z0cGdvY2l3OHNXRUNzZmdkZDI5Zkgybndoa0I= # Base64-encoded value
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: sftpgo
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  ports:
    - port: 21
      targetPort: 2121
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp
    - port: 30000
      targetPort: 30000
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp-passive0
    - port: 30001
      targetPort: 30001
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp-passive1
    - port: 30002
      targetPort: 30002
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp-passive2
    - port: 30003
      targetPort: 30003
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp-passive3
    - port: 30004
      targetPort: 30004
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp-passive4
    - port: 30005
      targetPort: 30005
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp-passive5
  selector:
    app: sftpgo
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: sftpgo
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: sftpgo
  replicas: 1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: sftpgo
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: sftpgo
          image: drakkan/sftpgo:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 2121
            - containerPort: 8080
              name: web-ui
          envFrom:
            - configMapRef:
                name: sftpgo-config
            - secretRef:
                name: sftpgo-secret
          volumeMounts:
            - name: sftpgo-data-volume
              mountPath: /srv/sftpgo/data
              subPath: sftpgo-data
            - name: certs-volume
              mountPath: /srv/sftpgo/certs
              readOnly: true
      volumes:
        - name: certs-volume
          secret:
            secretName: sftpgo-web-ui-tls
        - name: sftpgo-data-volume
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: sftpgo-data-pvc

---
# Important to let the volume claim outside of the deployment to prevent delete it in the case of deployment deletion
# As it will store the ftp data we need to keep it
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-data-pvc
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
  storageClassName: sbs-default-retain
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: db-sftpgo
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: db-sftpgo
  serviceName: "db-sftpgo"
  replicas: 1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: db-sftpgo
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: postgres-sftpgo
          image: postgres:16
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: sftpgo
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: sftpgo
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: sftpgo-secret
                  key: SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__PASSWORD
          volumeMounts:
            - name: postgres-data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
              subPath: postgres-data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: postgres-data
      spec:
        accessModes: [ "ReadWriteOnce" ]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 10Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: db-sftpgo
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  ports:
    - port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
      protocol: TCP
  selector:
    app: db-sftpgo
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-web-ui
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  ports:
    - port: 8080
      targetPort: 8080
      protocol: TCP
      name: web-ui
  selector:
    app: sftpgo
---
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-web-ui
  namespace: egreen
spec:
  entryPoints:
    - web
  routes:
    - match: Host(`ftp.k8-test.egreen.fr`)
      kind: Rule
      middlewares:
        - name: redirect-https
      services:
        - name: sftpgo-web-ui
          port: 8080
---
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-web-ui-tls
  namespace: egreen
spec:
  entryPoints:
    - websecure
  routes:
    - match: Host(`ftp-ui.k8-test.egreen.fr`)
      kind: Rule
      services:
        - name: sftpgo-web-ui
          port: 8080
  tls:
    secretName: sftpgo-web-ui-tls

---
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Certificate
metadata:
  name: ftp-ui.k8-test.egreen.fr
spec:
  dnsNames:
    - ftp-ui.k8-test.egreen.fr
    - ftp.k8-test.egreen.fr
  secretName: sftpgo-web-ui-tls
  issuerRef:
    name: letsencrypt-prod # Use the issuer created previously
    kind: ClusterIssuer
```

In the container of sftpgo I have e permission error to write in the volume sftpgo data
The permission error you're encountering when trying to write to the `sftpgo-data` volume in the SFTPGo container is likely due to a mismatch between the user or group running the container and the permissions on the mounted volume. Here's how you can troubleshoot and resolve this issue:

---

### 1. **Check the User Running the SFTPGo Container**
The `drakkan/sftpgo` container runs as a specific user by default. According to the [SFTPGo documentation](https://github.com/drakkan/sftpgo), the container typically runs as the `sftpgo` user with a specific UID and GID (e.g., `1000:1000`).

You can verify the user running the container by checking the Dockerfile or by running the following command inside the container:

```bash
kubectl exec -it <sftpgo-pod-name> -- id
```

This will show the UID and GID of the user running the container.

---

### 2. **Check the Volume Permissions**
The PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC) you created (`sftpgo-data-pvc`) is mounted to the container at `/srv/sftpgo/data`. If the volume's permissions do not match the UID and GID of the container's user, the container will not be able to write to the volume.

To check the permissions of the mounted volume, you can inspect it from within the container:

```bash
kubectl exec -it <sftpgo-pod-name> -- ls -ld /srv/sftpgo/data
```

If the owner or group of the directory does not match the UID and GID of the container's user, you will need to adjust the permissions.

---

### 3. **Fix the Permissions**
There are several ways to fix the permissions issue:

#### a. **Change the Ownership of the Volume**
You can manually change the ownership of the mounted volume to match the UID and GID of the container's user. For example:

```bash
kubectl exec -it <sftpgo-pod-name> -- chown -R 1000:1000 /srv/sftpgo/data
```

Replace `1000:1000` with the actual UID and GID of the container's user if it's different.

#### b. **Use an Init Container to Set Permissions**
You can use an init container to set the correct permissions on the volume before the main container starts. Update your `Deployment` to include an init container like this:

```yaml
initContainers:
        - name: init-permissions
          image: busybox
          command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /srv/sftpgo/data"]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: sftpgo-data-volume
              mountPath: /srv/sftpgo/data
```

This ensures that the permissions are set correctly every time the pod starts.

#### c. **Run the Container as Root (Not Recommended)**
As a last resort, you can run the container as the `root` user to bypass permission issues. However, this is not recommended for security reasons. To do this, update your `Deployment` to include the following:

```yaml
      securityContext:
        runAsUser: 0
        runAsGroup: 0
```

---

### 4. **Verify the Fix**
After applying one of the fixes above, restart the pod and verify that the container can write to the volume:

```bash
kubectl delete pod <sftpgo-pod-name>
```

Then check the logs of the SFTPGo container to ensure there are no permission errors.

---

### 5. **PersistentVolume Reclaim Policy**
Ensure that the `PersistentVolume` backing your `PersistentVolumeClaim` has a `ReclaimPolicy` of `Retain`. This prevents the volume from being deleted when the PVC is deleted. You can check this with:

```bash
kubectl get pv
```

If the `ReclaimPolicy` is not `Retain`, you may need to update it.

---

### 6. **StorageClass Considerations**
Some storage classes automatically set permissions on volumes. If you're using a dynamic provisioner, check the documentation for your storage class to see if it supports setting default permissions or ownership.

---

By following these steps, you should be able to resolve the permission error and allow the SFTPGo container to write to the `sftpgo-data` volume.
